| July–September 1863 | Lieutenant Governor Gordon encourages Maritime union | [Arthur Gordon](/wiki/Arthur_Hamilton-Gordon,_1st_Baron_Stanmore "Arthur Hamilton-Gordon, 1st Baron Stanmore"), newly appointed British [lieutenant governor](/wiki/Lieutenant_Governor_of_New_Brunswick "Lieutenant Governor of New Brunswick") of [New Brunswick](/wiki/New_Brunswick "New Brunswick"), encourages [Samuel Leonard Tilley](/wiki/Samuel_Leonard_Tilley "Samuel Leonard Tilley"), [premier of New Brunswick](/wiki/Premier_of_New_Brunswick "Premier of New Brunswick"), and [Charles Tupper](/wiki/Charles_Tupper "Charles Tupper"), [premier of Nova Scotia](/wiki/Premier_of_Nova_Scotia "Premier of Nova Scotia"), to consider the possibility of a union of the three Maritime provinces: New Brunswick, [Nova Scotia](/wiki/Nova_Scotia "Nova Scotia"), and [Prince Edward Island](/wiki/Prince_Edward_Island "Prince Edward Island")[[123]](#cite_note-127) |
| March 28, 1864 | Nova Scotia resolution for Maritime union conference | Premier Tupper introduces resolution in [Nova Scotia House of Assembly](/wiki/Nova_Scotia_House_of_Assembly "Nova Scotia House of Assembly") to appoint delegates to a conference of the three Maritime provinces to consider the possibility of Maritime union; union only to occur if approved by statutes passed by each of the three provinces and the Queen; resolution passes with all-party support[[124]](#cite_note-128)[[125]](#cite_note-129) |
| April 9, 1864 | New Brunswick resolution for Maritime union conference | Premier Tilley introduces resolution in [New Brunswick House of Assembly](/wiki/Legislative_Assembly_of_New_Brunswick "Legislative Assembly of New Brunswick") to appoint delegates to a conference of the three Maritime provinces to consider the possibility of Maritime union; union only to occur if approved by statutes passed by each of the three provinces and the Queen; resolution passes with all-party support[[126]](#cite_note-130)[[127]](#cite_note-131) |
| April 18, 1864 | Prince Edward Island resolution for Maritime union conference | [John Hamilton Gray](/wiki/John_Hamilton_Gray_(Prince_Edward_Island_politician) "John Hamilton Gray (Prince Edward Island politician)"), [premier of Prince Edward Island](/wiki/Premier_of_Prince_Edward_Island "Premier of Prince Edward Island"), introduces resolution in [Legislative Assembly of Prince Edward Island](/wiki/Legislative_Assembly_of_Prince_Edward_Island "Legislative Assembly of Prince Edward Island") to appoint delegates to a conference of the three Maritime provinces to consider the possibility of Maritime union; no further action to be taken until report of the Conference be laid before the Prince Edward Island Legislative Assembly; resolution passes on party lines[[128]](#cite_note-132)[[129]](#cite_note-133) |
| June 14, 1864 | Report on constitutional reform in [Province of Canada](/wiki/Province_of_Canada "Province of Canada") | [George Brown](/wiki/George_Brown_(Canadian_politician) "George Brown (Canadian politician)"), member of the [Legislative Assembly of the Province of Canada](/wiki/Legislative_Assembly_of_the_Province_of_Canada "Legislative Assembly of the Province of Canada"), presents committee report addressing flaws in the constitutional system of the Province of Canada; report favours a federal system of government, either for the two sections of the Province of Canada alone, or for a union of the British North American provinces[[130]](#cite_note-134)[[131]](#cite_note-135) |
| June 14, 1864 | Government of the Province of Canada falls | The same day Brown presents the report, the government falls on a [non-confidence motion](/wiki/Non-confidence_motion "Non-confidence motion"); stark illustration of the political instability of the Province of Canada; second government to fall in 1864, after only two and a half months in office[[132]](#cite_note-136) |
| June 14–16, 1864 | Brown initiates discussions with [John A. Macdonald](/wiki/John_A._Macdonald "John A. Macdonald") | Political overtures by Brown to [John A. Macdonald](/wiki/John_A._Macdonald "John A. Macdonald"), [George-Étienne Cartier](/wiki/George-%C3%89tienne_Cartier "George-Étienne Cartier") and [Alexander T. Galt](/wiki/Alexander_T._Galt "Alexander T. Galt") to seek constitutional changes; Macdonald responds; Brown favours federal constitution for Province of Canada; Macdonald, Cartier and Galt propose seeking union of all eastern British North American provinces[[133]](#cite_note-137) |
| June 17–30, 1864 | [Great Coalition](/wiki/Great_Coalition "Great Coalition") formed | Coalition government of [Liberal-Conservatives](/wiki/Liberal-Conservative_Party#History "Liberal-Conservative Party") from Canada West (led by Macdonald); [Reformers](/wiki/Reform_movement_(Upper_Canada) "Reform movement (Upper Canada)") from Canada West (led by Brown); *[Bleus](/wiki/Parti_bleu "Parti bleu")* from Canada East (led by Cartier); and Liberal-Conservatives from Canada East (led by Galt); Coalition agrees to pursue union of eastern British North American provinces; failing that, will seek a federal constitution for the Province of Canada[[134]](#cite_note-138) |
| June 30, 1864 | Canadians ask to attend conference on Maritime Union | [Governor General](/wiki/Governor-General_of_the_Province_of_Canada "Governor-General of the Province of Canada") [Monck](/wiki/Charles_Monck,_4th_Viscount_Monck "Charles Monck, 4th Viscount Monck") sends letters to the Maritime lieutenant governors, requesting that the Province of Canada be permitted to send a delegation to the upcoming conference on Maritime union[[135]](#cite_note-139) |
| September 1–9, 1864 | [Charlottetown Conference](/wiki/Charlottetown_Conference "Charlottetown Conference"), [Charlottetown](/wiki/Charlottetown "Charlottetown"), Prince Edward Island | Meeting of delegates from Province of Canada, Nova Scotia, New Brunswick and Prince Edward Island; no real discussion of Maritime union; Province of Canada proposal for a union of the British North American provinces gains general support; Conference delegates agree to continue discussions at Quebec; Maritime Union shelved[[136]](#cite_note-140)[[137]](#cite_note-141) |
| October 10–27, 1864 | Quebec Conference, Quebec City, Province of Canada | Delegates from Province of Canada, Nova Scotia, New Brunswick, Prince Edward Island and [Newfoundland](/wiki/Newfoundland_Colony "Newfoundland Colony") meet in Quebec to discuss the Confederation proposal in more detail; Conference passes the [Quebec Resolutions](/wiki/Quebec_Resolutions "Quebec Resolutions"), which outline a detailed proposal for Confederation of the British North American provinces[[138]](#cite_note-142)[[139]](#cite_note-143)[[140]](#cite_note-144) |
| October 19, 1864 | [St. Albans Raid](/wiki/St._Albans_Raid "St. Albans Raid") | Group of Confederate soldiers travel to Canada and conduct a cross-border raid to St. Albans, Vermont; captured by Canadian authorities; judge in Montreal rejects extradition application and releases them; episode creates considerable tension with the United States government |
| October–December 1864 | Cabinet crisis in Prince Edward Island | Prince Edward Island cabinet splits over the Quebec Resolutions and Confederation; Attorney General [Edward Palmer](/wiki/Edward_Palmer_(Canadian_politician) "Edward Palmer (Canadian politician)"), delegate to both the conferences, challenges the proposals; Premier Gray, who supports Confederation, resigns[[141]](#cite_note-145) |
| 1865 | | |
| Date | Event | Result |
| January 7–9, 1865 | New premier in Prince Edward Island | [James Colledge Pope](/wiki/James_Colledge_Pope "James Colledge Pope"), opposed to Confederation, becomes premier of Prince Edward Island[[142]](#cite_note-146) |
| February 3, 1865 | Confederation Debates begin in Province of Canada | Lengthy debates begin in the Parliament of the Province of Canada on the merits of the Confederation project[[143]](#cite_note-147) |
| February 6, 1865 | Confederation discussed in Newfoundland | Newfoundland premier [Hugh Hoyles](/wiki/Hugh_Hoyles "Hugh Hoyles") states in debates that Confederation would not be rushed through the Legislative Assembly[[144]](#cite_note-148) |
| February–March 1865 | New Brunswick election | Pro-Confederation government of Premier Tilley defeated by Anti-Confederation group; Anti-Confederation leader, [Albert James Smith](/wiki/Albert_James_Smith "Albert James Smith"), becomes premier[[145]](#cite_note-149) |
| February 20, 1865 | Confederation Debates in Province of Canada | Quebec Resolutions approved by Legislative Council by vote of 45 to 15[[146]](#cite_note-150) |
| March 2, 1865 | Confederation discussed in Prince Edward Island | Premier Pope states in the Legislative Assembly that any Confederation plan would be put to the voters, and that his government does not support Confederation[[147]](#cite_note-151) |
| March 6, 1865 | Newfoundland postpones decision | Premier Hoyles proposes to the Legislative Assembly that no decision be taken on the Quebec Resolutions until after the upcoming Newfoundland election[[148]](#cite_note-152) |
| March 10, 1865 | Conclusion of Confederation Debates in Province of Canada | Quebec resolutions approved by Legislative Assembly by vote of 91 to 33[[149]](#cite_note-153) |
| March 24–31, 1865 | Confederation debates in Legislative Assembly of Prince Edward Island | Premier J.C. Pope leads the Anti-Confederation position in the debates; his brother, [William Henry Pope](/wiki/William_Henry_Pope_(Canadian_politician) "William Henry Pope (Canadian politician)"), leads the Pro-Confederation position; Assembly rejects Confederation by vote of 23 to 5[[150]](#cite_note-154) |
| April 10, 1865 | Maritime union raised again in Nova Scotia | Considerable opposition to Confederation in Nova Scotia; Premier Tupper introduces motion for re-consideration of Maritime union as a stopgap measure[[151]](#cite_note-155) |
| May 1865 | Canadian delegation to Britain | Macdonald, Cartier, Galt and Brown travel to Britain to discuss defence of the Province of Canada, now that the US Civil War is over; no firm commitment from British government[[152]](#cite_note-156) |
| June 24, 1865 | Pressure from Britain | The [Colonial Secretary](/wiki/Secretary_of_State_for_the_Colonies "Secretary of State for the Colonies"), [Edward Cardwell](/wiki/Edward_Cardwell,_1st_Viscount_Cardwell "Edward Cardwell, 1st Viscount Cardwell"), sends a dispatch to the three Maritime provinces, urging them to accept Confederation to aid imperial defence[[153]](#cite_note-157) |
| July 1865 | Britain urges Confederation | Anti-Confederation premier Smith of New Brunswick and [William Annand](/wiki/William_Annand "William Annand"), a member of the Anti-Confederation group in the Legislative Assembly of Nova Scotia, travel separately to London to express dissatisfaction with the Confederation proposal; they each meet with Cardwell, the Colonial Secretary; Cardwell advises them that the British government strongly favours Confederation along the lines of the Quebec Resolutions, and will do everything in its power to achieve Confederation[[154]](#cite_note-158) |
| November 6, 1865 | York by‑election, New Brunswick | Vacancy in the New Brunswick Assembly forces Anti-Confederation government to call [by-election](/wiki/By-election "By-election") in York [riding](/wiki/Riding_(division)#Canada "Riding (division)"); [Charles Fisher](/wiki/Charles_Fisher_(Canadian_politician) "Charles Fisher (Canadian politician)"), former premier, delegate to Quebec, and strong supporter of Confederation, wins by-election[[155]](#cite_note-159) |
| November 7, 1865 | Newfoundland election | The leaders of the two parties in the Newfoundland election, [Frederick Carter](/wiki/Frederick_Carter "Frederick Carter") and Ambrose Shea, had both been delegates to Quebec and support Confederation; Carter wins the election, but overall, the majority of the members of the Assembly do not support Confederation[[156]](#cite_note-160) |
| 1866 | | |
| Date | Event | Result |
| February 20, 1866 | Newfoundland postpones decision | In first session after the 1865 election, the Newfoundland Legislative Assembly votes to delay any decision on Confederation[[157]](#cite_note-161) |
| March 12, 1866 | Governor General Monck intervenes | Monck sends a telegram to Lieutenant Governor Williams, suggesting that Williams make overtures to leader of the Anti-Confederates[[158]](#cite_note-162) |
| March 13, 1866 | Lieutenant Governor of Nova Scotia suggests conference | Lieutenant Governor Williams summons Annand, leader of the Anti-Confederates in the Assembly, and suggests that Annand propose a new conference, in London, under the supervision of the Imperial government[[159]](#cite_note-163) |
| April 4, 1866 | Anti-Confederation proposal for London Conference | William Miller, a leading Anti-Confederate in the Nova Scotia Assembly, proposes that there be another conference, in London[[160]](#cite_note-164) |
| April 6, 1866 | Legislative Council of New Brunswick supports Confederation | The Legislative Council of New Brunswick votes in favour of Confederation and the Quebec Resolutions[[161]](#cite_note-165)[[162]](#cite_note-166) |
| April 10, 1866 | Nova Scotia proposal for London Conference | Premier Tupper introduces resolution stating that Confederation is desirable, and therefore the Assembly authorises the lieutenant governor "to appoint delegates to arrange with the Imperial Government a scheme of union which will effectually ensure just provision for the rights and interest of this Province..."[[163]](#cite_note-167)[[164]](#cite_note-168) |
| April 12–13, 1866 | Resignation of Anti-Confederation government of New Brunswick | Premier Smith and his government resign as a result of Lieutenant Governor Gordon accepting the resolution of the Legislative Council, approving of Confederation; Lieutenant Governor Gordon appoints [Peter Mitchell](/wiki/Peter_Mitchell_(politician) "Peter Mitchell (politician)"), a supporter of Confederation and delegate to the Quebec Conference, as premier[[165]](#cite_note-169) |
| April 17, 1866 | Tupper's resolution passes | Nova Scotia Assembly passes Tupper's resolution proposing a conference in London, by a vote of 31 to 19[[166]](#cite_note-170)[[167]](#cite_note-171) |
| May 7–8, 1866 | Prince Edward Island rejects Confederation | Further debate in the Prince Edward Island Legislative Assembly; clear rejection of Confederation[[168]](#cite_note-172) |
| May–June 1866 | [New Brunswick election](/wiki/1866_New_Brunswick_general_election "1866 New Brunswick general election") | Lieutenant Governor Gordon dissolves the Assembly on advice of the new government; Pro-Confederation group wins elections, with majority of 33 seats compared to 8 seats for Anti-Confederation group[[169]](#cite_note-173) |
| June 30, 1866 | New Brunswick supports London Conference | The New Brunswick Legislative Assembly passes a Resolution to appoint delegates for the London Conference to discuss the union of the colonies, under the auspices of the Imperial government, "upon such terms as will secure the just rights and interests of New Brunswick", including a guarantee for the inter-colonial railway[[170]](#cite_note-174) |
| 1866 | Last session of Parliament of Province of Canada | Legislative Assembly of Province of Canada passes resolutions setting out proposed constitutions for Ontario and Quebec[[171]](#cite_note-175) |
| December 4–23, 1866 | [London Conference](/wiki/London_Conference_of_1866 "London Conference of 1866") begins | Delegates from Province of Canada, Nova Scotia and New Brunswick meet in London to review and revise the Quebec Resolutions; revisions include guarantee of the inter-colonial railway and strengthening provisions for denominational and separate schools[[172]](#cite_note-176) |
| December 24, 1866 | London Conference concludes | Delegates unanimously approve modified resolutions; Macdonald transmits them to the new Colonial Secretary, [Lord Carnarvon](/wiki/Henry_Herbert,_4th_Earl_of_Carnarvon "Henry Herbert, 4th Earl of Carnarvon"), for consideration[[173]](#cite_note-177) |
| 1867 | | |
| Date | Event | Result |
| January–February 1867 | Drafting of the bill | Committee of the delegates begin the drafting process to implement the London Resolutions; extensive consultations with Lord Carnarvon and British drafter; bill goes through several drafts[[174]](#cite_note-178) |
| February–March 1867 | Bill passed by British Parliament | Lord Carnarvon introduces the *British North America Act, 1867* in the House of Lords; Lord Monck speaks in support; Cardwell, now in opposition, speaks in support in the Commons; bill proceeds through the Lords and the Commons without incident[[175]](#cite_note-179) |
| March 29, 1867 | [Queen Victoria](/wiki/Queen_Victoria "Queen Victoria") grants [Royal Assent](/wiki/Royal_Assent "Royal Assent") | *British North America Act, 1867* enacted as Imperial statute |
| July 1, 1867 | Proclamation of *British North America Act, 1867* | Canada is created |
| July 1, 1867 | Macdonald appointed first prime minister of Canada | Governor General Monck appoints Macdonald as first prime minister of Canada; Macdonald then sets up the first federal government, appointing the federal Cabinet and the lieutenant governors of the four provinces.[[176]](#cite_note-180) |
| July 1, 1867 | Continuation of New Brunswick government | Premier Mitchell continues in office as the first post-Confederation premier of New Brunswick |
| July 4, 1867 | Continuation of Nova Scotia government | Lieutenant Governor Williams appoints [Hiram Blanchard](/wiki/Hiram_Blanchard "Hiram Blanchard") as first post-Confederation premier of Nova Scotia, after Premier Tupper resigns to stand for election to the federal [House of Commons](/wiki/House_of_Commons_of_Canada "House of Commons of Canada") |
| July 15, 1867 | Creation of first Quebec government | [Lieutenant Governor Belleau](/wiki/Narcisse-Fortunat_Belleau "Narcisse-Fortunat Belleau") appoints [Pierre-Joseph-Olivier Chauveau](/wiki/Pierre-Joseph-Olivier_Chauveau "Pierre-Joseph-Olivier Chauveau") as first premier of Quebec |
| July 16, 1867 | Creation of first Ontario government | [Lieutenant Governor Stisted](/wiki/Henry_William_Stisted "Henry William Stisted") appoints [John Sandfield Macdonald](/wiki/John_Sandfield_Macdonald "John Sandfield Macdonald") as first premier of Ontario |
| August–September 1867 | First elections under the *British North America Act, 1867* | Elections for [federal Parliament](/wiki/1867_Canadian_federal_election "1867 Canadian federal election"), Legislative Assemblies of [Ontario](/wiki/1867_Ontario_general_election "1867 Ontario general election"), [Quebec](/wiki/1867_Quebec_general_election "1867 Quebec general election") and [Nova Scotia](/wiki/1867_Nova_Scotia_general_election "1867 Nova Scotia general election") (no election in New Brunswick since there had been an election the previous year) |
